title: Prime Minister Takashi plans to reduce the consumption tax on food by August, facing business opposition
summary: The Japanese government will decide the policy for reducing the consumption tax on food in early August, while the presidents of Suntory and Asahi oppose the measure.

title: Agreed route for Hokuriku Shinkansen extension with station in Katsuragawa, Kyoto
summary: The government and parties agreed on the Obama-Kyoto route for the bullet train extension, with a construction period of 26 years and the new Katsuragawa station.

title: England and Argentina face off in the World Cup semifinals after 24 years
summary: The historic rivalry between England and Argentina revives in the football World Cup semifinals, with extensive media coverage and global anticipation.

title: Promising Japanese baseball player Rintaro Sasaki signs with the Miami Marlins
summary: Rintaro Sasaki, selected in the MLB draft, chose to join the Miami Marlins instead of playing in the NPB with the SoftBank Hawks.

title: Former students ordered to pay compensation for death due to school bullying in Yamagata
summary: The court ordered three former students to pay full compensation for the death of a classmate crushed under a mat, in a bullying case that has reached three rulings.

title: Chito Kosukawa and Kasumi Asakura win the 2025 Akutagawa and Naoki prizes
summary: The 175th edition of the prestigious Japanese literary awards recognized Chito Kosukawa for 'The Zombie Collector' and Kasumi Asakura for 'Kengwai'.

title: Japanese imperial family rests at the Nasu Imperial Villa
summary: Emperor Naruhito, Empress Masako, and Princess Aiko enjoy their stay at the imperial villa, strolling in casual attire.

title: Japan defeats Italy in the Volleyball Nations League and achieves nine consecutive victories
summary: The Japanese men's volleyball team defeated Italy in five sets, securing their place in the final round of the tournament with an undefeated streak.

title: Ex-nurse arrested for introducing feces into a patient's intravenous drip causing his death
summary: A former nursing assistant was arrested for allegedly mixing feces into the serum of a hospitalized patient, who died the following day; the suspect denies the charges.

title: Reform of the Imperial House Law advances amid opposition criticism
summary: The bill to reform the Imperial House Law continues its progress in the Diet, with the opposition denouncing it as an attack on the imperial system, and its approval is expected on the 17th.

title: Spain eliminates France in the World Cup semifinals with a solid defense
summary: The Spanish football team defeated France, standing out for its defensive organization that neutralized Mbappé, eliminating the French from the tournament.

title: Eight-year-old boy dies hit by a car on a pedestrian crossing in Western Tokyo
summary: A third-grade student was fatally run over by a car while crossing a pedestrian crossing with a green light in the city of Nishi-Tokyo.

title: Woman found dead on a barge in Osaka; investigated as possible crime
summary: A woman was found dead on a barge under the Namiwaya Bridge in Osaka, and authorities are investigating the circumstances, considering the possibility of a crime.

title: Cyberattack on Nichirei causes disruptions in restaurants and food manufacturers
summary: The food logistics company Nichirei suffered a cyberattack that affected its systems, impacting chains like Kura Sushi and other clients.

title: Lower House approves 'subcapital' bill, criticized for lack of preparation
summary: The Japanese House of Representatives approved the bill to designate a subcapital, with criticism that the proposal is insufficiently developed, and the government seeks a majority in the Upper House.

title: Trump proposes toll in the Strait of Hormuz and withdraws it after criticism; attacks Iran for five days
summary: The US president suggested charging 20% to ships crossing the Strait of Hormuz, but withdrew the idea after pressure and launched airstrikes on Iran.

title: UNESCO considers Japan's explanation about the Sado gold mine insufficient
summary: The UNESCO World Heritage Committee published a draft resolution asking Japan to improve the presentation of the complete history of the Sado gold mine.

title: Euro cuts losses against the dollar, yen appreciates slightly
summary: In London, the euro reduced its losses against the dollar and the pound rose; in Tokyo, the yen gained six sen against the dollar, reflecting movements in currency markets.

title: Japanese and US stock markets rise on lower expectations of rate hikes
summary: The Nikkei index rose more than 1000 points for the second consecutive day, the TOPIX also advanced, and the Dow Jones opened higher as expectations of rate hikes in the US decrease.

title: United Kingdom restricts social media use by minors and TikTok reinforces election monitoring
summary: The British government plans to limit nighttime access to social media for teenagers, while TikTok announces continuous monitoring of election-related posts.
